sections.title: Authorization Request Header
Job Description
sections.generation_prompt: Generate a clinical narrative describing the patient's job. Use proper title format (Mr./Ms./Mrs.). Start with job title and employer, then work schedule. Include physical job requirements if available. Use past tense 'worked as' format. Be concise and professional.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Mr. Anderson states he worked as a Site Inspector for Smith Emery Company. He worked 8 hours per day, 5 days per week.
Mr. Anderson states his job entailed prolonged driving, walking, standing, squatting, twisting, bending forward, side bending, reaching below shoulder level, reaching above shoulder level, pushing, pulling, gripping, grasping, lifting up to a maximum of 40 lbs, and carrying up to

Ms. Bachar states she worked as an On-Site Property Manager for Euro Cabinet Design & Woodworking. She worked 24 hours per day, 7 days per week.
Ms. Bachar states her job entailed prolonged walking, standing, squatting, twisting, bending forward, side bending, reaching below shoulder level, reaching above shoulder level, pushing, pulling, gripping, grasping, lifting up to a maximum of 25 lbs, and

sections.title: Current Work Status
sections.generation_prompt: Generate a single sentence describing current work status. Use proper title format (Mr./Ms./Mrs.). For 'Disabled/Not Working', include stop date if available. For 'Working for another employer', mention this. For 'Still working same employer', state this clearly.

few_shot_examples.output_text: Mr. Anderson states that he has not returned to work since November 5, 2021.
Mr. Canseco Gutierrez states that he is working for another employer as a driver.

sections.title: History of Industrial Injury
sections.generation_prompt: Generate clinical narrative about industrial injury. Start with injury date and circumstances. Use specific dates or cumulative trauma periods. Include how orthopedic pain developed into clenching/bruxism. Mention repetitive work activities for CT cases. Use proper clinical terminology.

input_fields.p6_injury_description_1: slipped and fell, landing in a splits position; his left leg struck a pile of rebar

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Mr. Anderson states that on November 5, 2021, he was involved in an industrial injury in the course of performing his usual and customary job duties.
Mr. Anderson reports that he slipped and fell, landing in a splits position; his left leg struck a pile of rebar. He complains of residual pain in his left elbow, lower back, left leg, left knee and in his left ankle.

Ms. Bachar states that from CT 1/1/10 to 2/28/23, she was involved in industrial injuries in the course of performing her usual and customary job duties.
Ms. Bachar reports that as a result of performing repetitive work activities such as prolonged walking, standing, squatting, twisting, bending forward, side bending, reaching below shoulder level, reaching above shoulder level, pushing, pulling

sections.title: Treatment Received for Industrial Injury
sections.generation_prompt: Generate narrative listing treatments received for industrial injury. Include physical therapy, chiropractic, acupuncture, surgery as applicable. Use proper clinical format. If no treatment, state 'has received no treatment'.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Mr. Anderson underwent left ankle surgery in 2024.
Mr. Anderson has received treatments of physical therapy.
Mr. Canseco Gutierrez has received no treatment.

sections.title: Past Medical History
sections.generation_prompt: Generate clinical narrative of past medical history. Always include stomach acids (gastric reflux) as primary condition. Add other conditions like diabetes, thyroid problems, high blood pressure as applicable. Use clinical terminology and proper sentence structure.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Mr. Anderson reports having stomach acids coming up into his mouth.
He is allergic to Cortisone and Sulfa.

Ms. Benton reports having diabetes and thyroid problems, as well as stomach acids coming up into her mouth.
She has undergone sleep study testing.

sections.title: Prior Surgeries
sections.generation_prompt: Generate clinical narrative of prior surgeries. If specific surgeries listed, include timeframes. If 'None' or empty, state 'cannot recall prior surgeries' or 'denies prior surgeries'. Use past tense and clinical format.

input_fields.p8_past_surgeries: right inguinal hernia repair about 30 years ago

few_shot_examples.output_text: Mr. Anderson underwent a right inguinal hernia repair about 30 years ago.

input_fields.p8_past_surgeries: right knee surgery, cesarean section, rhinoplasty, hysterectomy

few_shot_examples.output_text: Ms. Benton underwent a right knee surgery, a cesarean section, a rhinoplasty, and a hysterectomy.

sections.title: Prior Injuries
sections.generation_prompt: Generate clinical narrative of prior injury history. Include both industrial and non-industrial injuries. If 'None', state 'cannot recall prior injuries'. Include recovery status when mentioned. Add disclaimer for injuries outside dental expertise.

few_shot_examples.output_text: Mr. Anderson cannot recall sustaining any prior injuries.

input_fields.p8_history_non_industrial_injuries: motor vehicle accident about 35 years ago, injured neck and back, made full recovery

few_shot_examples.output_text: Ms. Boricean was involved in a motor vehicle accident about 35 years ago and injured her neck and back areas. She had since made a full recovery.

sections.title: Subsequent Injuries
sections.generation_prompt: Generate clinical narrative about injuries after industrial date. If 'None', state 'has not been involved in any injuries subsequent to the industrial injury'. If injuries present, describe with timeframe.

few_shot_examples.output_text: Mr. Anderson states he has not been involved in any injuries subsequent to the industrial injury.

sections.title: Social History
sections.generation_prompt: Generate smoking history narrative. If both smoking fields are 'NO', state 'does not smoke and has never been a smoker'. If current smoker, include cigarettes per day and duration. If former smoker, include quit timeframe and previous smoking history.

few_shot_examples.output_text: Mr. Anderson does not smoke and has never been a smoker.
Ms. Bachar has smoked approximately 1-2 cigarettes per day for the past 30 years.

sections.title: Past Dental History
sections.generation_prompt: Generate dental history narrative. Include last dentist visit timeframe, dentist name if available, and services received (cleaning, x-rays). End with standard request for prior dental records for comprehensive apportionment assessment.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Mr. Anderson states he was last seen by a dentist approximately 1 month prior to this examination.
His private dentist is Mary Chester.
He states that he had x-rays taken at his last dental visit, and his teeth cleaned about 3-4 years prior to this examination.
I welcome the provision of Mr. Anderson's prior dental x-rays and records for the purpose of providing a comprehensive assessment of apportionment once he reaches Permanent and Stationary status.

sections.title: Subjective Complaints
sections.generation_prompt: Generate detailed subjective complaint narrative. Include headache patterns (location, frequency, severity, VAS scores), facial pain patterns, and dental/bruxism complaints in response to orthopedic pain. Use structured format with clear headings for Headaches, Facial Pain, and Dental sections.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Headaches
Occasional slight headaches in the bilateral temple and forehead areas
Headache pain described as sharp and dull
Headaches occurring about 1% of the time
Headache pain intensity of 4-5 on a Visual Analog Scale of 0 to 10
Dental
Clenching and grinding his teeth and bracing his facial musculature in response to the industrial related orthopedic pain and resultant emotional stressors

sections.title: Oral Malodor Complaint
sections.generation_prompt: Generate bad breath complaint narrative since industrial injuries. Include intensity VAS, percentage of time present, embarrassment level, and social interference. Use clinical format with VAS scoring.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Ms. Benton states that since the industrial injuries she has experienced bad breath.
Ms. Benton rates the intensity of her bad breath as a 6 on a VAS.
Ms. Benton states that her bad breath is present 60% of the time.
Ms. Benton rates the amount of her bad breath causing her to be embarrassed as a 10 on a VAS.

sections.title: Epworth Sleepiness Scale
Clinical Examination of the Temporomandibular Joint
sections.generation_prompt: Generate TMJ examination findings narrative. Start with 'Physical examination revealed the following:'. Describe crepitus, clicking, and joint noises during specific movements (translational, lateral). Include palpation pain findings for lateral condyles if VAS > 0.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Physical examination revealed the following:
Crepitus noises palpated and auscultated in the right Temporomandibular Joint upon translational and lateral movements of the mandible

sections.title: Clinical Examination of the Muscular System
sections.generation_prompt: Generate muscle examination narrative describing taut bands and trigger points in temporalis and masseter muscles. Include statement about pain reproduction when palpating taut bands. Use clinical terminology for bilateral findings.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Subjective pain was elicited and objective palpable taut bands were found upon palpation of the:
Right masseter muscle
Left masseter muscle
Mr. Anderson informed us that when we objectively palpated the taut band in his masseter muscles on the right and left sides, this reproduced his facial pain complaint.

sections.title: Mandibular Range of Motion
Intraoral Examination
Objective Clinical Findings Confirming Bruxism/Clenching
sections.generation_prompt: Generate bruxism findings narrative. Include tongue scalloping (bilateral), buccal mucosal ridging of cheeks, and occlusal wear patterns. Use clinical terminology and describe severity/location of findings.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Teeth indentations/Scalloping of the right and left lateral borders of the tongue
Bite mark line/Buccal mucosal ridging of the inner right and left cheeks
Wear on the surfaces of the teeth

sections.title: Ultrasonic Doppler Auscultation Analysis
sections.generation_prompt: Generate ultrasonic findings narrative. Start with 'The recorded objective data verified and confirmed:'. Describe crepitus sounds in specific TMJ locations during translational and lateral movements. Include confirmation or denial of internal derangements.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
The recorded objective data verified and confirmed:
Crepitus sounds were ultrasonically auscultated in the right Temporomandibular Joint upon translational and lateral movements of the mandible

sections.title: Surface Electromyography (sEMG)
sections.generation_prompt: Generate sEMG findings narrative. State that studies were performed on masseter, anterior temporalis, sternocleidomastoid, and trapezius muscles. Confirm elevated muscular activity and incoordination/aberrant function of facial musculature.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
sEMG studies were performed on the masseter, anterior temporalis, sternocleidomastoid, and trapezius muscles and confirmed:
Elevated muscular activity; with incoordination and aberrant function of the facial musculature was confirmed

sections.title: Diagnostic Bite Force Testing
sections.generation_prompt: Generate bite force testing narrative explaining the procedure using gnathodynamometer, comparing actual measurements to normal values (400+ Newtons), and describing functional implications of reduced bite force in relation to trigeminal nerve function.

few_shot_examples.output_text: The muscles of mastication were tested for their functional capacity measuring the maximum bite force and strength of these facial muscles. The measurements were objectively determined with the use of a gnathodynamometer. These measurements objectively determine the maximum bite force of the muscles of mastication used in chewing, comparing one side to the other. The muscles of mastication are innervated by the trigeminal nerve.

sections.title: Diagnostic Autonomic Nervous System Testing
sections.generation_prompt: Generate autonomic testing narrative describing pulse oximetry during Mueller Maneuver. Include heart rate changes and whether results are within normal limits. Add alpha-amylase enzyme analysis section with KIU/L measurements.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Diagnostic Autonomic Nervous System Testing, consisting of Pulse Oximetry during the patient's performance of the Mueller Maneuver, revealed results within normal limits.
DIAGNOSTIC α-AMYLASE ENZYME ANALYSIS
Diagnostic α-Amylase Analysis, consisting of laboratory spectrophotometric analysis of the amount of α-amylase enzyme present, was performed, and an α-amylase enzyme concentration of 114 KIU

sections.title: Diagnostic Salivary Flow and Buffering Tests
sections.generation_prompt: Generate salivary testing narrative describing observable dry mouth, lip condition, saliva quality (viscous, cloudy, ropey), and tongue depressor adherence test results. Reference ADA standard of care test.

few_shot_examples.output_text:
Diagnostic salivary flow and buffering tests revealed:
Observable dry mouth, dry lips, cracked lips
Viscous saliva
The standard of care objective test advocated by the American Dental Association to prove whether definite qualitative changes of the saliva are present or not was performed (and the results photographed). A tongue depressor adheres to the inside of the patient's cheeks, which conclusively

sections.title: Diagnostic Direct Fluorescence Visualization (DFV)
sections.static_content: Stress, as well as the side effect of medications, causes xerostomia which manifests as qualitative changes of the saliva. Saliva in its proper form is anti-mutagenic which means it helps decrease the onset of oral cancer. When saliva has been quantitatively changed due to the side effect of medications and/or due to the anti-cholinergic side effect of stress, it has been scientifically documented

sections.title: Diagnosis of Industrial Related Conditions
sections.template:
After a review of {patient_title} {patient_last_name}'s history and clinical examination, the diagnoses are:
{diagnosis_list}

field_id_glossary.p7_orthopedic_pain_clenching: Description of orthopedic pain leading to clenching
field_id_glossary.p3_interfere_others_after: Bad breath interference with others VAS
field_id_glossary.p14_right_lateral_pole_vas: Right TMJ lateral pole pain VAS
field_id_glossary.p14_left_lateral_pole_vas: Left TMJ lateral pole pain VAS
field_id_glossary.p14_right_lateral_mm: Right lateral jaw movement in millimeters
field_id_glossary.p14_left_lateral_mm: Left lateral jaw movement in millimeters
field_id_glossary.p16_right_newtons: Right side bite force in Newtons
field_id_glossary.p16_left_newtons: Left side bite force in Newtons

generation_notes:
These rules generate comprehensive clinical dental reports for workers' compensation cases. The report follows a standardized structure with both required universal sections and conditional sections based on clinical findings. Key features include:

1. Formatted header with claim information
2. Narrative sections using clinical tone and proper medical terminology  
3. Conditional sections that appear only when relevant findings are present
4. ADL table with difficulty severity levels
5. Comprehensive diagnostic testing results
6. ICD-10 diagnosis codes with clinical descriptions

The rules handle missing data gracefully and maintain consistent clinical language throughout. Special attention is given to bruxism/clenching as the primary industrial-related dental condition, with supporting diagnostic findings from various tests and examinations.
